title: Mega Manectric Battle Guide
summary: An [Electric] special [sweeper] that opens with [in[timid]ate] on entry, then runs the meta down with 135/135 SpA and Speed.
strengths: Post-Mega 135/135 SpA and Speed put it in the top Speed tier
[in[timid]ate] drops oppo[sing] Atk on every entry
[volt-switch] enables a constant [pivot] and chip plan
weaknesses: Thin 70/80/80 defenses fold to one retaliation hit
The Mega slot is locked — no alternative item plans

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Electric] STAB with clean finishing power

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Manectric combines [in[timid]ate], 135 SpA, and 135 Speed into one of the cleanest [electric] [pivot]s in the format. Base Manectric sits at a mediocre 105/105 line, but the Mega pushes both stats into the top of the Speed tier and simultaneously drops oppo[sing] Attack on every entry. Because pure [Electric] has a [sing]le weakness to manage, Mega Manectric slots neatly into any cycle team as a pressure-and-[pivot] engine that rarely loses a turn.

## Strengths
135 Speed outruns virtually every unboosted attacker without needing cho[ice]-scarf, and [volt-switch] [recycle]s [in[timid]ate] on every rotation, slowly grinding down physical-offense teams match after match. [thunderbolt] two-shots the [Water] and [Flying] cores that the metagame leans on, while [flamethrower] punishes the [Steel] and [Grass] [pivot]s (notably Venusaur and Garchomp [counter]s) that would otherwise wall the plan. The pre-Mega [in[timid]ate] turn also bakes in a free matchup advantage before the actual Mega Evolution takes effect.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ground] is still a 2x weakness, and any [earthquake] or [earth-power] user threatens an immediate KO. Mega Manectric's 70/80/80 defensive line is on the fragile side for a Mega — one clean retaliation will remove half of its HP. Priority attackers like [bullet-punch] and [ice]-shard users plus cho[ice]-scarf [Dragon]s are the main [revenge] threats, so do not try to stay in when those mons are telegraphed. Tera [Ice] flips the [Ground] weakness, while Tera [Electric] maxes STAB damage.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is locked in as the main STAB. [volt-switch] is the heart of the cycle plan because it maintains momentum while letting [in[timid]ate] reset on every [pivot]. The third slot is [flamethrower], which punishes [Steel] and [Grass] switch-ins who try to wall the STAB combo. The last slot is [hidden-power] [Ice], which hits [Ground] and [Dragon] [pivot]s that would otherwise be safe against the cove[rage] triangle. [overheat] and [thunder-wave] are valid swaps depending on whether the team wants a nuker or a status spreader.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[manectite] is the only item — no alternative exists. [in[timid]ate] locks in automatically after Mega Evolution, so the pre-Mega ability is effectively irrelevant. [timid] is the standard nature to max out the Speed tier. For Tera, [Electric] deepens [thunderbolt] output for maximum offensive ceiling, while [Ice] is the defensive flip that removes the [Ground] weakness and turns Mega Manectric into a surprise answer for [Ground]-type [sweeper]s.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Grass] and [Water] partners remove the [Ground] threats that Mega Manectric cannot handle directly. A [Water] [pivot] paired with Mega Manectric creates an infinite [volt-switch] + U-turn cycle that grinds most offense into dust. A [taunt] user on the back line prevents oppo[sing] setup [sweeper]s from snowballing, and a dedicated priority cleaner handles the late-game finishing work once Mega Manectric has chipped the oppo[sing] team down.

## Play Patterns
Always enter on a turn where [in[timid]ate] matters — the free physical debuff is the reason Mega Manectric exists. After that, rotate via [volt-switch] to [protect] its thin bulk and reuse [in[timid]ate] repeatedly. C[lick] [thunderbolt] only for confirmed KOs so you [minimize] retaliation exposure. Save the Tera trigger for a telegraphed [earthquake] or priority move that would otherwise remove Mega Manectric from the field.
